title: Gopichand on Badminton's Big Changes and CWG Exclusion
indian_angle: Pullela Gopichand, a prominent Indian badminton figure, discusses the sport's exclusion from the 2026 Commonwealth Games and other major rule changes.
summary: Pulleela Gopichand addresses badminton's absence from the 2026 Glasgow Commonwealth Games and shares his perspective on significant rule changes impacting the sport.

title: PV Sindhu Wins Japan Open, Silences Doubters After Emotional Comeback
indian_angle: Indian star PV Sindhu secured a significant title at the Japan Open, marking an emotional return to form after facing criticism.
summary: PV Sindhu clinched the Japan Open title, overcoming a period of self-doubt and criticism, a victory she described as bringing tears to her eyes and sparked by a cryptic remark from Virat Kohli.

title: Inside PV Sindhu’s Radical, Data-Driven Comeback to Top Form
indian_angle: This feature details the scientific approach and data analysis used by sports scientist Wayne Lombard to help Indian badminton stalwart PV Sindhu regain her peak performance.
summary: Sports scientist Wayne Lombard reveals the radical, data-driven strategies employed to reengineer PV Sindhu's game, leading to her recent Japan Open triumph and return to top form.

title: Devika, Prannoy Exit China Masters on Opening Day
indian_angle: Indian shuttlers Devika and HS Prannoy faced early exits in their respective categories at the China Masters tournament.
summary: Devika, the Thailand Open Super 300 champion, lost a tough three-game match, while HS Prannoy also exited the China Masters on the first day of competition.

title: BAI Releases Eligibility Criteria for World Junior Championships 2026
indian_angle: Badminton Association of India (BAI) has issued guidelines for player selection for the upcoming World Junior Championships in Cairo, Egypt.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has released the official player eligibility criteria for selection to the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, to be held in Cairo, Egypt.

title: 3x15 Scoring System Implemented in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has implemented a new 3x15 scoring system for domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in junior categories.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships across U-13, U-15, and U-17 age groups.
